turns.content:
You are the subagent Name: gsd-planner, role: phase planning specialist for the Hivemind harness project. You must do as this prompt instructed and knowing that you are being delegated by hm-l0-orchestrator.

## Goal

Plan the 5 new REQs (REQ-58-07..10 + REQ-58-META) added to P58's SPEC in commit 34a24fce. Produce a comprehensive executable plan that, when executed, actually fixes the 4 user-visible symptoms and implements the 3 process changes.

## Inputs

1. **Updated SPEC:** `.planning/phases/58-tmux-orchestration-programmatic-pool-interactive-delegate-cl/58-SPEC.md` (469 lines, commit 34a24fce) — read FULL, especially lines 184-290 (5 new REQs)
2. **META-ANALYSIS:** `.planning/phases/58-tmux-orchestration-programmatic-pool-interactive-delegate-cl/58-META-ANALYSIS.md` (231 lines) — read FULL
3. **Team diagnosis 1 (mine):** `.planning/debug/p58-symptom-diagnosis-2026-06-04.md`
4. **Team diagnosis 2 (other):** `.planning/debug/tmux-delegate-streaming-gaps.md`
5. **Current CONTEXT:** `.planning/phases/58-tmux-orchestration-programmatic-pool-interactive-delegate-cl/58-CONTEXT.md` (re-validated, 17 decisions D-58-01..17)
6. **Original PLANS:** `.planning/phases/58-tmux-orchestration-programmatic-pool-interactive-delegate-cl/58-PLAN-01..07.md` (skim, understand existing patterns and conventions)

## Required Output

Write to: `.planning/phases/58-tmux-orchestration-programmatic-pool-interactive-delegate-cl/58-PLAN-08-GAP-FIX.md`

### Section 1: Plan Overview
- Plan name: 58-PLAN-08-GAP-FIX
- Scope: 4 symptoms (S1-S4) + 3 process changes
- Estimated tasks: ~25-35 atomic commits
- Wave structure: 4 waves (RED tests → implementation → integration → META)
- Verification gate: REAL UAT (human-driven, per REQ-58-META AC-04)
- Invariants preserved: 27-tool-key, P20 no-new-deps, AC#10/AC#11 manualOverride checks

### Section 2: Architecture Decisions

For each of the 5 REQs, lock the implementation approach:

**S1 (REQ-58-07): capture-pane polling in tmux integration**
- File: `src/features/tmux/tmux-multiplexer.ts` (add `capturePaneContent(paneId)` method)
- File: `src/features/tmux/session-manager.ts` (add polling loop, 5-10s interval)
- File: `src/tools/delegation/delegation-status.ts` (add `peek` action to Zod union, action enum line 25)
- File: `tests/scripts/tmux/67-panel-live-update.bats` (new BATS)
- Why capture-pane over SDK event subscription: capture-pane is SDK-agnostic, works regardless of opencode attach state. SDK event subscription requires SDK signature verification (high risk for 27-tool-key).

**S2 (REQ-58-08): widen permission gate for take-over/release/peek**
- File: `src/tools/tmux-copilot.ts` (lines 51-56, 175-180) — add user-tier for `take-over`, `release`, `peek` actions
- File: `src/tools/tmux-copilot.ts` (add new action `peek` for user-tier)
- File: `tests/scripts/tmux/68-user-inject.bats` (new BATS)
- Why widen over new user-facing tool: lower risk to 27-tool-key invariant, preserves existing tool surface

**S3 (REQ-58-09): keep-alive mechanism**
- File: `src/coordination/delegation/manager-runtime.ts:244` — change `await sendPromptAsync` to `void sendPromptAsync(...).catch(...)`
- File: `src/coordination/delegation/coordinator.ts` — add post-dispatch callback that injects a synthetic tool call (delegation-status auto-poll) into orchestrator's context
- File: `src/tools/delegation/delegate-task.ts:32` — fix the comment "always-background WaiterModel" to match code (or fix code to match comment)
- File: `tests/scripts/tmux/69-stream-stays-open.bats` (new BATS)
- Why void + auto-poll over explicit keep-alive tool: preserves 27-tool-key, no new tool surface

**S4 (REQ-58-10): real-time child event streaming**
- File: `src/coordination/delegation/manager-runtime.ts` (after sendPrompt) — add SDK event subscription if available
- File: `src/features/session-tracker/index.ts` — extend `SessionTracker` to subscribe to child events
- File: `src/tools/delegation/delegation-status.ts` — add `progress` action returning live counters
- File: `tests/scripts/tmux/70-progress-mid-flight.bats` (new BATS)
- Why event subscription in existing path: preserves 27-tool-key (no new tool)

**REQ-58-META: 3 process changes**
- File: `.claude/get-shit-done/templates/spec.md:25-99` — add `## User-Pain Coverage` section template
- File: `.claude/get-shit-done/templates/verification.md` — add `## Human-Driven UAT` section template
- File: `.planning/ROADMAP.md` — add `## Symptom Coverage Matrix` section
- File: `.planning/USER-PAIN-BACKLOG.md` — create new file, list 4 open symptoms (S1-S4) with status
- File: `.planning/phases/58-tmux-orchestration-programmatic-pool-interactive-delegate-cl/58-VERIFICATION-EXTEND.md` — REAL UAT evidence for P58 gap-fix (human-driven, not BATS)

### Section 3: Task List (Atomic, Per-Task Commits)

Group tasks into 4 waves. Each task = 1 atomic commit. List tasks as:

Wave 1: RED Tests (must fail before any implementation)
- Task W1-T1: Write BATS test 67-panel-live-update.bats
- Task W1-T2: Write BATS test 68-user-inject.bats
- Task W1-T3: Write BATS test 69-stream-stays-open.bats
- Task W1-T4: Write BATS test 70-progress-mid-flight.bats

Wave 2: Implementation (after all RED tests verified)
- W2-T1 to W2-T12: For each REQ, the implementation tasks (4-6 per REQ)

Wave 3: Integration (BATS re-run, all green, regression checks)
- W3-T1: Full BATS 62-70 all green
- W3-T2: 27-tool-key invariant check
- W3-T3: AC#10/AC#11 manualOverride regression
- W3-T4: 3,310 vitest pass
- W3-T5: tsc --noEmit clean

Wave 4: META process changes
- W4-T1: Add User-Pain Coverage section to spec template
- W4-T2: Add Human-Driven UAT section to verification template
- W4-T3: Add Symptom Coverage Matrix to ROADMAP
- W4-T4: Create USER-PAIN-BACKLOG.md

### Section 4: Dependency Order

Critical sequencing:
- All RED tests MUST be written and failing before any implementation starts
- Implementation tasks can be parallelized within a wave IF they don't share file ownership
- S1 and S4 both modify src/coordination/delegation/manager-runtime.ts — must be SEQUENTIAL, not parallel
- S2 and S3 modify different files — can be parallel
- META changes are independent of code changes — can run parallel with Wave 2/3
- Final integration wave: BATS 62-70 all green + 27-tool-key check + manualOverride regression + tsc clean + 3,310 vitest pass

### Section 5: Verification Plan

Per-task verification:
- Each implementation task: BATS re-run for relevant test, must pass
- After Wave 2 (all impl done): full BATS suite 62-70 must pass
- After Wave 3 (integration): regression checks
  - 27-tool-key invariant: tests/integration/hook-registration.test.ts:86-103 6/6 PASS
  - AC#10: src/plugin.ts:923-926 appendTuiPrompt manualOverride check
  - AC#11: src/tools/tmux-copilot.ts:264-275 forward-prompt manualOverride check FIRST
  - 3,310 vitest pass
  - tsc --noEmit clean
- After Wave 4 (META): templates updated, ROADMAP matrix added, USER-PAIN-BACKLOG created
- GATE 4 (REAL UAT, per REQ-58-META AC-04): HUMAN-DRIVEN UAT, not BATS. User tests the 4 symptoms in real runtime. PASS or PARTIAL-with-follow-up required to ship.

### Section 6: Risk Register

| Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|-----------|--------|------------|
| 27-tool-key invariant broken | MEDIUM | CRITICAL (P20 l0-orchestrator contract) | Each impl task reviewed against tool-key matrix; prefer in-place SDK augmentation over new tools |
| SDK signature mismatch (S4) | MEDIUM | HIGH | Wrap SDK calls in try/catch; fallback to no-op if SDK doesn't support event subscription |
| capture-pane expensive (S1) | MEDIUM | MEDIUM | Cap content size (e.g., last 100 lines), implement exponential backoff |
| void sendPrompt loses error (S3) | MEDIUM | MEDIUM | Add pre-send validation; log on send failure; surface in delegation record |
| BATS 61 pre-existing failure | LOW | LOW | Not P58 regression; flag in plan but do not block |
| REAL UAT cancel/disconnect | HIGH | HIGH | Multiple UAT attempts with timeout, user-acknowledged UAT cancellation pattern |

### Section 7: Atomic Commit Strategy

Each task = 1 commit. Commit message format: phase-58-gap-fix(<scope>): <description>

Scopes: S1 | S2 | S3 | S4 | META | test | docs

Examples:
- phase-58-gap-fix(S1): add capturePaneContent to TmuxMultiplexer
- phase-58-gap-fix(S1): add pane content polling loop to SessionManager
- phase-58-gap-fix(S1): expose peek action on delegation-status
- phase-58-gap-fix(S1): add BATS 67-panel-live-update (RED-first)
- phase-58-gap-fix(S2): add user-tier permission gate for take-over/release/peek
- phase-58-gap-fix(S2): add peek action to tmux-copilot for user-session
- phase-58-gap-fix(S2): add BATS 68-user-inject (RED-first)
- phase-58-gap-fix(S3): convert sendPromptAsync to fire-and-forget
- phase-58-gap-fix(S3): add auto-poll keep-alive to coordinator
- phase-58-gap-fix(S3): fix delegate-task.ts:32 comment to match code
- phase-58-gap-fix(S3): add BATS 69-stream-stays-open (RED-first)
- phase-58-gap-fix(S4): add SDK event subscription to dispatch path
- phase-58-gap-fix(S4): expose progress action on delegation-status
- phase-58-gap-fix(S4): add BATS 70-progress-mid-flight (RED-first)
- phase-58-gap-fix(META): add User-Pain Coverage section to spec template
- phase-58-gap-fix(META): add Human-Driven UAT section to verification template
- phase-58-gap-fix(META): add Symptom Coverage Matrix to ROADMAP
- phase-58-gap-fix(META): create USER-PAIN-BACKLOG.md with 4 open symptoms

## Constraints

- DO NOT implement anything (no code changes, no test changes)
- ONLY write to .planning/phases/58-*/58-PLAN-08-GAP-FIX.md
- Mark as L5 documentation
- Cite source files for each task
- Use atomic commit strategy (one commit per task)
- RED-FIRST protocol: BATS tests must fail before implementation
- 27-tool-key invariant must be preserved (no new tool keys, only new actions on existing tools)
- P20 invariant: no new package.json deps
- AC#10 and AC#11 manualOverride checks must continue to pass

## Return Format

After writing the plan, return:
- File path
- New line count
- Total tasks count
- Wave structure
- Estimated atomic commits
- Risk register count
- Confirmation ready for plan-check (gsd-plan-checker) then execution (gsd-execute-phase)

Tôi là **subagent gsd-planner**, phase planning specialist cho Hivemind harness. Tôi sẽ đọc các input files, verify approach với codebase thực tế, sau đó viết plan `58-PLAN-08-GAP-FIX.md` tuân thủ RED-FIRST, atomic commits, 27-tool-key invariant.
Tôi đã ghi nhận `.planning/AGENTS.md` (L5 docs guidance, planning sector, naming + date-stamp convention). Tôi sẽ tiếp tục đọc các file input còn lại, verify đường dẫn templates, rồi viết plan.
